reasoning: The Metaculus community prediction stands at 73%, but objective statistical models from climate science groups provide a much lower probability. Specifically, Berkeley Earth's updated model for January 2025 assigns only a 30% chance for 2025 to be the warmest year on record, while groups like the Met Office and Carbon Brief have 2025 centered as the third-warmest year. The year started strong, with January 2025 being the warmest January in the Copernicus ERA5 dataset. However, February fell to the third-warmest on record, tracking 0.18°C behind February 2024. With an emerging La Niña bringing a cooling influence to 2025 (in contrast to the strong El Niño that boosted early 2024), 2025 is mathematically disadvantaged to maintain the anomalous warmth needed to outpace 2024's record for the rest of the year. Due to these structural factors and explicit expert forecasts, I am predicting significantly lower than the community, anchoring closer to Berkeley Earth's formal estimate but leaving some room for continued anomalous baseline warming that has characterized 2023-2024.
